stimulus: As a furniture designer in Chicago in the 1960s, Helen Sutton became known for avoiding ______blank designs in her work. Instead of using standard wooden shapes and muted colors, she typically experimented with curving forms and bright finishes.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice D is correct because the contrast with experimenting with curving forms and bright finishes requires a word meaning ordinary or standard. "Conventional" fits. Choices A, B, and C all describe the kind of design Sutton actually preferred, so she wouldn’t avoid them.
